title: Reed Jobs would rather talk about curing cancer than his last name
summary:
Here's the extracted information in a clean, highly condensed bulleted list:

**Core Story/Main Event:**
- Reed Jobs, son of Steve Jobs, is the founder of Yosemite, an oncology-focused venture firm launched in 2023.
- Yosemite aims to build biotech companies from scratch using a mix of philanthropy and outside investment capital.

**Important Names, Companies, or People:**
- Reed Jobs (founder of Yosemite)
- Steve Jobs (Reed's father)
- Connie Loizos (author of the article)
- Maggie (producer)
- Jennifer Doudna (lab recipient of a grant from Yosemite)
- Craig Crews (serial founder and co-founder of Quarry)
- Eli Lilly (pharmaceutical company)
- Revolution Medicines (biotech company)
- Amgen (biotech company)
- Tune Therapeutics (epigenetic editing company)
- Histosonics (device company)
- Pfizer (pharmaceutical company)

**Pricing, Release Dates, or Financial Numbers:**
- $350 million: target for Yosemite's second fund
- 40%: proposed cut of the NIH budget (rejected by the Senate and House)
- 12%: proposed cut of the NIH budget (rejected by the Senate and House)
- $100 billion: proposed increase in NIH funding
- $260 million: cost of a Phase 3 cancer trial
- 1%: percentage of people who spontaneously clear hepatitis B
- 25: number of companies in Yosemite's portfolio
- 2: number of companies that haven't worked out for scientific reasons
- $7 billion: price of Eli Lilly's acquisition of Kelonia
- 15%: percentage of the genome that could be targeted by drugs historically
- 90%: approval rate of NIH funding

**Product Specifications, Hardware Details, or Software Features:**
- Epigenetic gene editing technology
- Safe delivery of gene editing to specific cells
- Induced proximity therapeutic approach
- Histotripsy device for liver tumor destruction
- Ultrasound technology for liver tumor destruction

**Direct Quotes:**
- "Are you kidding?" (Reed Jobs, on using a MacBook)
- "One of extreme activity right now" (Reed Jobs, on the state of Yosemite)
- "We think we found something to hit that exposed [marker] across all the different ways p53 gets mutated" (Reed Jobs, on targeting p53)
- "Unfortunately, yes — I’ve seen companies with great science fail because of bad storytelling from the CEO" (Reed Jobs, on the importance of storytelling in biotech)
- "We now have the first trillion-dollar pharmaceutical company, Eli Lilly, because of GLP-1s — the best-selling drug class in the world" (Reed Jobs, on the impact of GLP-1s)

title: Here’s How Apple Is Updating Its Child Safety Features in iOS 27
summary:
Here's a highly condensed bulleted list of crucial facts extracted from the raw text:

**Core Story/Main Event:**
- Apple is updating its child safety features in iOS 27, iPad OS 27, and macOS 27.
- The updates come after years of advocacy from groups like Heat Initiative and lawsuits over child safety issues.

**Important Names, Companies, or People:**
- Sarah Gardner, founder and CEO of Heat Initiative
- Anunay Kulshrestha, applied cryptographer and information security consultant at Infosec Clinic
- Julian Chokkattu, Senior Editor, Gear at WIRED
- Joel Khalili, author of an article about the UK's ban on social media for kids under 16

**Pricing, Release Dates, or Financial Numbers:**
- No specific pricing or financial numbers mentioned.
- The updates will be available in iOS 27, iPad OS 27, and macOS 27, with a release date not specified.

**Product Specifications, Hardware Details, or Software Features:**
- Updates to Child Accounts:
  - Revamped onboarding process for creating a child account
  - Limiting adult websites
  - Setting age-appropriate media
  - Implementing age-based restrictions in the App Store
  - Option to choose what apps kids can access on the device
- Ask to Browse: a new experience in Safari that requires kids to ask parents for permission to visit a new website
- Communication Safety feature:
  - Automatically detects and blurs nudity in Messages, Face Time, and Air Drop for users under 18
  - Expanded to include gore or graphic violent content
  - Works in Shared Photo Albums, Contact Posters, and the Contacts app
- Time Allowances:
  - Suggestions on how much time kids should spend in specific app categories based on their age
  - Customizable by parents
  - Daily schedules to allow groups of apps at certain times of the day or week
- Screen Time interface:
  - Redesigned for an at-a-glance view that shows a kid's average device usage and most-used apps

**Direct Quotes:**
- "Overall, it was a huge win that they spent 10 minutes of the keynote addressing child safety, because that never would have happened a few years ago." - Sarah Gardner
- "Apple, as a company, tried to ignore for a long time that they were part of a child’s online experience at all. Echoes of ‘we’re just hardware’ come to mind when I think of Apple's approach to child safety." - Sarah Gardner
- "A government can pressure Apple into adding something to the set that isn't CSAM, and Apple is known to defer to governments." - Anunay Kulshrestha
- "We have a long-standing commitment to building a safe and trusted platform for kids, and provide many industry-leading tools that help keep them safe while also safeguarding their privacy." - Apple

title: Scientists’ Side Hustle? Using AI and Quantum Computing to Generate New Peptides
summary:
Here's a highly condensed bulleted list of crucial facts extracted from the raw text:

**Core Story/Main Event:**
- Scientists at the Technical University of Denmark (DTU) successfully used a quantum computer to improve the accuracy and reach of generative artificial intelligence (AI) drug discovery models.

**Important Names, Companies, or People:**
- Timothy Patrick Jenkins (DTU professor and project leader)
- Jonathan Funk (DTU Ph.D. student)
- Richard Murray (ORCA Computing chief executive officer)
- Novo Nordisk Foundation (funding organization)
- ORCA Computing (British startup)
- BP (oil major)
- Toyota (car manufacturer)

**Pricing, Release Dates, or Financial Numbers:**
- No specific pricing or financial numbers mentioned
- The project was funded by the Novo Nordisk Foundation

**Product Specifications, Hardware Details, or Software Features:**
- The quantum computer used was built by ORCA Computing and is printer-sized
- The hybrid technique used a combination of quantum and traditional processors
- The generative AI model was used to predict proteins and generate novel peptides

**Direct Quotes:**
- "Most innovative science is too scary for foundations," - Timothy Patrick Jenkins
- "I was a huge quantum skeptic" - Timothy Patrick Jenkins
- "Quantum is still not very powerful, so the level of complexity that we could encode wasn’t a normal-sized antibody, which is what we usually work with," - Jonathan Funk
- "We needed to really prove it to convince skeptics that our predictions connect to the real world," - Timothy Patrick Jenkins
- "I think it’s no surprise that lots of industrial companies think quantum is hazy and far away," - Richard Murray
- "We needed this as an easy way to validate that now we actually have a shot at moving the needle substantially," - Timothy Patrick Jenkins

title: The correct way to position your router's antennas depends on your home
summary:
Here's the extracted information in a clean, structured bulleted list:

**Core Story/Main Event:**
- The correct way to position a router's antennas depends on the home's layout and can significantly impact Wi-Fi signal strength, coverage range, and dead zones.

**Important Names, Companies, Brands, or Key People:**
- SOLDATOOFF (photographer)
- Jakub Zerdzicki (photographer)
- TP-Link (router manufacturer)

**Complete List of Products/Features:**
- **Router Antennas:**
  - Most router antennas are omnidirectional, broadcasting in all directions at the same time.
  - Vertical antennas point straight up, radiating signal outward horizontally.
  - Horizontal antennas push the signal upward and downward, useful for homes with multiple floors.
- **Router Frequencies:**
  - 2.4 GHz: provides slower speeds, but longer range and better wall penetration.
  - 5 GHz: hits better speeds but is more susceptible to physical obstructions, while covering a smaller area.
  - 6 GHz (newer tri-band routers): provides a massive speed boost with near-zero interference, but struggles to travel far and penetrate solid walls.
- **Router Placement:**
  - Central location: essential for optimal signal coverage.
  - Height: 1 to 1.5 feet off the ground, aligning the signal with most devices.
  - Avoid placing the router near:
    - Microwave ovens
    - Fish tanks
    - Bluetooth devices
    - Metal objects
    - Thick concrete walls

**Pricing, Release Dates, or Financial Numbers:**
- No specific pricing or financial information is mentioned in the text.

**Direct Quotes:**
- "The router's antenna position directly affects Wi-Fi signal strength, coverage range, and dead zones throughout the home." (Original phrasing: "The router's antenna position directly affects Wi-Fi signal strength, coverage range, and dead zones throughout the home.")
- "Fix the antenna direction, place the router in the right place and at the right height, and you'll see quite the difference." (Original phrasing: "Fix the antenna direction, place the router in the right place and at the right height, and you'll see quite the difference.")

title: Here's Why Apple is Reportedly Skipping M6 Pro and M6 Max Chips
summary:
Here's the extracted information in a clean, structured bulleted list:

* **Core Story/Main Event:**
 + Apple is reportedly skipping the M6 Pro and M6 Max chips.
* **Important Names, Companies, Brands, or Key People:**
 + Mark Gurman (Bloomberg)
 + Apple
* **Complete List of Products/Features:**
 + M6 chip
 + M6 Pro chip (skipped)
 + M6 Max chip (skipped)
 + M7 chip
 + M7 Pro chip
 + M7 Max chip
 + M7 Ultra chip
 + M5 chip
 + M5 Pro chip
 + M5 Max chip
 + M5 Ultra chip
* **Pricing, Release Dates, or Financial Numbers:**
 + M5 chip: October 2025
 + M5 Pro and M5 Max chips: March 2026
 + M5 Ultra chip: Late 2026
 + M6 chip: Late 2026
 + M7 chip: First half of 2027
 + M7 Pro and M7 Max chips: Second half of 2027
 + M7 Ultra chip: 2028
* **Direct Quotes:**
 + "Apple had been planning major neural-processing upgrades for the M7 family and ultimately decided those improvements were important enough to justify accelerating the next generation rather than completing the M6 lineup." - Mark Gurman
 + "AI is no longer just another feature Apple's chips need to support. It is now shaping how those products are designed and when they are shipped." - Mark Gurman
 + "The M7 Ultra chip in particular 'dramatically upgrades AI performance,' and that it may power Apple Intelligence servers starting in 2029." - Mark Gurman

title: Power of Apple's M7 & M8 chips was born from Apple Car research
summary:
Here's the extracted information in a clean, structured bulleted list:

* **The Core Story / Main Event:**
  + Apple Car research is being repurposed to improve artificial intelligence performance in the M7 and M8 processors.
  + The M7 and M8 processors are expected to be used in the 16-inch MacBook Pro and Apple Intelligence servers.

* **Important Names, Companies, Brands, or Key People:**
  + Apple
  + Apple Insider
  + Bloomberg

* **Complete List of Products/Features:**
  + M7 Pro processor (for the 16-inch MacBook Pro)
  + M7 processor (for Mac)
  + M8 processor (for Apple Intelligence servers)
  + Apple Intelligence servers

* **Pricing, Release Dates, or Financial Numbers:**
  + No specific pricing information is mentioned.
  + No specific release dates are mentioned.
  + Apple Car research investment: $10 billion over 10 years.

* **Direct Quotes:**
  + None mentioned in the text.

* **Additional Facts:**
  + Apple Car was cancelled after 10 years of development.
  + Apple Car was initially considered Apple's biggest failure.
  + Apple Insider argued that Apple Car research would pay off.
  + Bloomberg reports that Apple is concentrating more on AI support than on overall speed and power efficiency in the M7 and M8 processors.
